perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall have the right to, and agrees that it will, use Discloser’s Confidential Information solely for the performance of its obligations and exercise of its rights under the License Agreement (the “Purpose”), except as may be otherwise specified in a separate definitive written agreement negotiated and executed between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall have the right to use Discloser’s Confidential Information for purposes related to the License Agreement, except as may be otherwise specified in a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific language 'solely for the performance of its obligations and exercise of its rights under the License Agreement (the “Purpose”)', we are creating ambiguity regarding how the recipient can use the confidential information, which is contradicted by the later section about restrictions in Section 4. The new clause states, the Recipient shall have the right to use Discloser’s Confidential Information for purposes related to the License Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the term of the License Agreement and a period of [***] thereafter (and indefinitely with respect to any individually identifiable health information disclosed by BIDMC to Licensee, if any), each Recipient agrees that: (i) it will not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than as specified herein; and (ii) it will use reasonable efforts (but no less than the efforts used to protect its own confidential and/or proprietary information of a similar nature) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any other person or entity except as expressly permitted hereunder or the License Agreement. Recipient may, however, disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only on a need-to-know basis to its and its Affiliates employees, staff members and agents (“Receiving Individuals”) who are directly participating in the Purpose and who are informed of the confidential nature of such information, provided Recipient shall be responsible for compliance by Receiving Individuals with the terms of this Agreement and any breach thereof Each party further agrees not to use the name of the other party or any of its Affiliates or any of their respective trustees, directors, officers, staff members, 19 [***] = CERTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT, MARKED BY BRACKETS, HAS BEEN OMITTED BECAUSE THE INFORMATION (I) IS NOT MATERIAL AND (II) WOULD BE COMPETITIVELY HARMFUL IF PUBLICLY DISCLOSED. employees, students or agents in any advertising, promotional or sales literature, publicity or in any document employed to obtain funds or financing without the prior written approval of the party or individual whose name is to be used, in the case of BIDMC such approval to be given by the Public Affairs Department. This Section 4 shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the recipient can use the confidential information 'solely for the performance of its obligations and exercise of its rights under the License Agreement'. The changed text broadens this to 'purposes related to the License Agreement,' removing the 'solely' limitation. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states 'it will not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than as specified herein' and further clarifies its usage based on the original 'Purpose'. The removal of 'solely' in the changed text allows for broader interpretation, directly conflicting with the strict limitations outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, including without limitation all written and tangible forms thereof, shall be and remain the property of the Discloser. Upon termination of this Agreement, if requested by Discloser, Recipient shall return or destroy at Discloser’s discretion all of Discloser’s Confidential Information, provided that Recipient shall be entitled to keep one copy of such Confidential Information in a secure location solely for the purpose of determining Recipient’s legal obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, including all forms thereof, shall be and remain the property of the Discloser.  Upon termination of this Agreement, if requested by Discloser, Recipient shall return or destroy at Discloser’s discretion all of Discloser’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'provided that Recipient shall be entitled to keep one copy of such Confidential Information in a secure location solely for the purpose of determining Recipient’s legal obligations hereunder,' an in-text contradiction is created. Section 10 mentions the Confidentiality Terms and Conditions survive any termination of the License Agreement. However, without the allowance to keep a copy of the confidential information as stated in Section 6, it would be hard to determine Recipient's legal obligations after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General . These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersede any prior oral or written understandings between the parties relating to confidential treatment of information. Sections 1, 2, 4, 7, 10 and 11 of these Confidentiality Terms and Conditions shall survive any expiration or termination of the License Agreement. 20 [***] = CERTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT, MARKED BY BRACKETS, HAS BEEN OMITTED BECAUSE THE INFORMATION (I) IS NOT MATERIAL AND (II) WOULD BE COMPETITIVELY HARMFUL IF PUBLICLY DISCLOSED.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the recipient to keep a copy of confidential information to determine legal obligations after termination. The changed text removes this allowance. The contradicted text states that the confidentiality terms and conditions survive termination of the license agreement. Without the ability to retain a copy of the confidential information, it becomes difficult, if not impossible, for the recipient to determine their legal obligations under the surviving terms, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement by it may cause irreparable harm to the other party and that each party is entitled to seek injunctive relief and any other remedy available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement by it may cause harm to the other party and that each party is entitled to seek legal remedy.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'irreparable harm' and 'injunctive relief', the altered text weakens the remedies available to the parties in case of a breach. This creates an in-text contradiction because the standard for proving harm is now less stringent, which impacts the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations outlined earlier in the document. The removal of 'injunctive relief' specifically limits the type of remedies available, making enforcement of time-sensitive confidentiality clauses more difficult. A later breach would be less strongly enforced by the legal remedy specified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the term of the License Agreement and a period of [***] thereafter (and indefinitely with respect to any individually identifiable health information disclosed by BIDMC to Licensee, if any), each Recipient agrees that: (i) it will not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than as specified herein; and (ii) it will use reasonable efforts (but no less than the efforts used to protect its own confidential and/or proprietary information of a similar nature) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any other person or entity except as expressly permitted hereunder or the License Agreement. Recipient may, however, disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only on a need-to-know basis to its and its Affiliates employees, staff members and agents (“Receiving Individuals”) who are directly participating in the Purpose and who are informed of the confidential nature of such information, provided Recipient shall be responsible for compliance by Receiving Individuals with the terms of this Agreement and any breach thereof Each party further agrees not to use the name of the other party or any of its Affiliates or any of their respective trustees, directors, officers, staff members, 19 [***] = CERTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT, MARKED BY BRACKETS, HAS BEEN OMITTED BECAUSE THE INFORMATION (I) IS NOT MATERIAL AND (II) WOULD BE COMPETITIVELY HARMFUL IF PUBLICLY DISCLOSED. employees, students or agents in any advertising, promotional or sales literature, publicity or in any document employed to obtain funds or financing without the prior written approval of the party or individual whose name is to be used, in the case of BIDMC such approval to be given by the Public Affairs Department. This Section 4 shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'irreparable harm' and 'injunctive relief,' weakening the remedies available. This contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines confidentiality obligations that benefit from the stronger enforcement mechanisms (like injunctive relief) originally available. Without the prospect of injunctive relief, the confidentiality obligations are less effectively enforced, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersede any prior oral or written understandings between the parties relating to confidential treatment of information.
perturbation.changed_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the core understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and supersede any prior oral or written understandings between the parties relating to confidential treatment of information', we remove the assurance that this document is the definitive agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the omission suggests that prior agreements could still be relevant, undermining the document's authority. This contradicts the explicit statement in Section 1 that capitalized terms not defined in the Confidentiality Terms have the meanings ascribed to them in the License Agreement. This omission leads to uncertainty of which the definitive agreement for confidential treatment is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information . “Confidential Information” shall mean any information, including but not limited to data, techniques, protocols or results, or business, financial, commercial or technical information, disclosed by one Party (each a “Discloser” as applicable) to the other Party (each a “Recipient” as applicable) in connection with the terms of that certain Exclusive License Agreement dated December , 2016 (the “License Agreement”) and identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. Capitalized terms used in this Appendix that are not otherwise defined herein have the meanings ascribed in the License Agreement to which this Appendix is attached and made a part thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the confidentiality terms and license agreement supersede any prior understandings. The changed text removes this assurance, implying that prior agreements could still be relevant. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text (definition of Confidential Information) refers specifically to the 'Exclusive License Agreement dated December, 2016' as the basis for defining confidential information and capitalized terms. By removing the superseding clause, the modified text introduces uncertainty about whether this License Agreement is the definitive agreement governing confidential treatment, thus contradicting the clear reliance on it in the contradicted text. This meets the 'Omissions' - In Text Contradiction definition because essential language ensuring the document's authority is removed, creating a contradiction with the reliance on the License Agreement defined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may, however, disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only on a need-to-know basis to its and its Affiliates employees, staff members and agents (“Receiving Individuals”) who are directly participating in the Purpose and who are informed of the confidential nature of such information, provided Recipient shall be responsible for compliance by Receiving Individuals with the terms of this Agreement and any breach thereof
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information to its employees, staff members and agents (“Receiving Individuals”) who are informed of the confidential nature of such information, provided Recipient shall be responsible for compliance by Receiving Individuals with the terms of this Agreement and any breach thereof
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'only on a need-to-know basis' and 'Affiliates... who are directly participating in the Purpose' creates a contradiction with the overall intent of confidentiality. Section 4 no longer defines who or when the recipient can share the discloser's information with. This could create an in-text contradiction because the level of confidentiality is ambiguated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exclusions . “Confidential Information” under this Agreement shall not include any information that (i) is or becomes publicly available through no wrongful act of Recipient; (ii) was known by Recipient prior to disclosure by Discloser, as evidenced by tangible records; (iii) becomes known to Recipient after disclosure from a third party having an apparent bona fide right to disclose it without any confidentiality obligation; (iv) is independently developed or discovered by Recipient without use of Discloser’s Confidential Information, as evidenced by tangible records; or (v) is disclosed to another party by Discloser without restriction on further disclosure. The obligations of confidentiality and non-use set forth in this Agreement shall not apply with respect to any information that Recipient is required to disclose or produce pursuant to applicable law, court order or other valid legal process provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser prior to such required disclosure, discloses such information only to the extent so required and cooperates reasonably with Discloser’s efforts to contest or limit the scope of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits disclosure to 'a need-to-know basis' by specific individuals. The changed text removes this restriction, allowing disclosure to a broader group of employees without the 'need-to-know' and 'directly participating in the Purpose' limitation. This contradicts the spirit of confidentiality implied in the 'Exclusions' section, where the intention is to limit what constitutes confidential information based on specific conditions (public availability, prior knowledge, etc.). While the Exclusions section doesn't directly state who the information can be shared with, the change introduces a scenario where confidential information could be more easily disclosed, potentially undermining the exclusions outlined in the Contradicted Text, making the exclusions less effective.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee will not disclose any export controlled information to BIDMC without the express prior written consent of BIDMC Technology Ventures Office. Licensee will indemnify BIDMC for any and all claims, actions, damages or liabilities of any kind related to Company’s failure to comply with this section.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee will indemnify BIDMC for any and all claims, actions, damages or liabilities of any kind related to Company’s failure to comply with export control laws.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific sentence 'Licensee will not disclose any export controlled information to BIDMC without the express prior written consent of BIDMC Technology Ventures Office', the contract omits a crucial protective measure for BIDMC regarding export-controlled information. While the clause retained still requires indemnification, the removal of the explicit prohibition on disclosure without consent weakens BIDMC's control over its confidential data and creates an in-text contradiction because the original text defines specific consent measures. Section 5, Right to Disclose, still states that the Discloser (BIDMC) has the right to disclose, but this is no longer assured because of the altered sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Right to Disclose . Discloser represents that to the best of its knowledge it has the right to disclose to each Recipient all of Discloser’s Confidential Information that will be disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited the Licensee from disclosing export-controlled information to BIDMC without prior written consent, thus ensuring BIDMC's right to control its confidential information. The changed text removes this explicit prohibition, weakening BIDMC's control. This contradicts the 'Right to Disclose' clause in Section 5, which states that BIDMC (Discloser) has the right to disclose confidential information, as BIDMC's ability to control and thus 'have the right' to disclose becomes uncertain without the explicit prohibition on the Licensee. The omission weakens the Discloser's power to disclose, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
